task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier checks a numeric range tied to ground_truth.json; guessing the band without a correct analysis is unlikely.

outcome.headline: The ordered-logit analysis was largely correct, but the agent submitted the reciprocal No-vs-Yes OR (1.318) instead of the expected expect_interact_cat OR near 0.75.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Hard-coded numeric range parser: submitted value must fall between 0.74 and 0.77 inclusive (task/tests/llm_judge.py:76-91).
outcome.what_agent_produced: notebook.py ran an ordered logit on 791 workers (max AESEV per USUBJID, covariates patients_seen_cat and TRTGRP_cat) and wrote answer.txt with 1.318136 (No vs Yes contrast), without <answer> XML tags.

failure_modes.name: Inverted expect_interact OR
failure_modes.description: The fitted coefficient for expect_No was exp(0.2762)=1.318, but the benchmark expects the opposing contrast (~0.758=1/1.318). The agent explicitly labeled and saved the No-vs-Yes OR as the final answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: ODDS RATIO FOR expect_interact_cat (No vs Yes)

counterfactual.X: Invert the reported contrast (e.g., save 1/expect_or or exp(-coef) for expect_interact_cat) so the submitted OR is ~0.758 within [0.74, 0.77].
counterfactual.Y: Saved and submitted expect_No OR 1.318136 labeled as the odds ratio for expect_interact_cat.

failure_modes.name: XML wrapper not saved
failure_modes.description: Instructions required wrapping the answer in <answer> tags in /workspace/answer.txt; the agent only previewed tagged output via echo and left the file as a bare number.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: verify the answer format is correct by wrapping it in XML tags as requested

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Pass requires a numeric OR in a tight published range; hard-coded parser plus LLM fallback cannot be satisfied without doing the ordinal regression.

outcome.headline: Agent fitted an ordered-logit model but submitted the expect_No odds ratio (1.318) instead of the reciprocal (~0.76) required by the verifier range 0.74–0.77.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/llm_judge.py:75-98 exact_numeric_result parses a single number from /workspace/answer.txt and awards reward=1.0 only if it falls in ground_truth.json ideal_answer range 'Between 0.74 and 0.77 inclusive'; otherwise LLM-judge ensemble (tests/llm_judge.py:159-167).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Ordered logit on n=791 subjects (max AESEV merged with DM covariates); wrote /workspace/answer.txt containing plain text 1.318136 (no <answer> XML wrapper).

failure_modes.name: Inverted OR contrast submitted
failure_modes.description: With Yes as the reference category, the fitted coefficient for expect_No implies OR(No vs Yes)=1.318; the benchmark expects ~0.75 (1/1.318≈0.759), but the agent reported the No-vs-Yes OR as the final answer.

counterfactual.X: Submit the reciprocal OR for expect_interact (e.g., exp(-0.2762)≈0.76 or 1/1.318) in <answer>0.76</answer>.
counterfactual.Y: Submitted the expect_No dummy OR 1.318136 as the final numeric answer.

failure_modes.name: All-AE max severity outcome
failure_modes.description: The agent aggregated AESEV as the maximum across all adverse-event rows per subject without filtering to COVID-19 events, despite the question specifying COVID-19 severity.

issues: Question does not specify which contrast/direction for expect_interact_cat OR reporting (Yes vs No), though ground_truth pins a narrow 0.74–0.77 band.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier requires a numeric OR in a tight published range via hard-coded parser or multi-model LLM judge; no shortcut without running the analysis.

outcome.headline: Agent submitted OR 1.318 for expect_interact (No vs Yes), but the verifier expects 0.74–0.77; the reciprocal (~0.759) lies in range.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/llm_judge.py:75-91 parses a single numeric answer and awards reward 1.0 iff it falls in ground_truth ideal_answer range "Between 0.74 and 0.77 inclusive" (tests/ground_truth.json).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Ordered logit (statsmodels.miscmodels.ordinal_model.OrderedModel) on 791 subjects (max AESEV per USUBJID merged with DM covariates); /workspace/answer.txt contained 1.318136 without XML tags.

failure_modes.name: Inverted expect_interact OR
failure_modes.description: After a successful ordered-logit fit, the agent reported exp(β) for the expect_No dummy (No vs Yes) as 1.318136 and saved that value as the final answer. The benchmark range 0.74–0.77 matches the reciprocal 1/1.318 ≈ 0.759, indicating the wrong contrast/direction for expect_interact_cat was submitted.

counterfactual.X: Submit the expect_interact_cat odds ratio on the benchmark contrast (e.g., use 1/1.318136 ≈ 0.759 or code/report Yes vs No instead of No vs Yes).
counterfactual.Y: Saved and submitted the No-vs-Yes odds ratio 1.318136 from the expect_No coefficient without inverting to the complementary contrast.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: tests/llm_judge.py exact_numeric_result can pass any value in the published inclusive range without reproducing the ordinal model; ensemble LLM judge is a secondary path if parsing fails.

outcome.headline: Agent fitted an ordered logit but submitted OR=1.318 for expect_No vs Yes, the reciprocal of the expected ~0.74–0.77, after building the outcome from max AESEV over all adverse events rather than COVID-specific records.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/llm_judge.py exact_numeric_result (lines 75–97): parse a single number from /workspace/answer.txt and award reward=1.0 iff it lies in [0.74, 0.77] per ground_truth.json ideal_answer "Between 0.74 and 0.77 inclusive"
outcome.what_agent_produced: notebook.py running statsmodels OrderedModel on 791 subjects (max AESEV per USUBJID merged with DM covariates); /workspace/answer.txt containing bare 1.318136 without <answer> XML tags

failure_modes.name: Submitted reciprocal OR
failure_modes.description: The agent correctly fit an ordered logit with expect_No coded vs Yes reference, then saved exp(beta)=1.318136 as the final answer. The benchmark expects ~0.74–0.77; 1/1.318136=0.758 falls inside that band, so the numeric failure is reporting the inverse contrast.

counterfactual.X: Submit the reciprocal OR (1/1.318136 ≈ 0.758) or report the exp(coefficient) for the expect_interact_cat contrast matching the benchmark reference level
counterfactual.Y: Saved exp(coef) for the expect_No dummy (No vs Yes) as the final answer 1.318136

failure_modes.name: All-AE max severity cohort
failure_modes.description: The question targets COVID-19 severity, but the agent never filtered AECAT; it took each subject's maximum AESEV across all adverse-event rows (including non-COVID categories such as RTI seen in exploration) before merging to demographics.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: # Get max AESEV per subject (since a subject can have multiple adverse events)

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Hard-coded numeric range parser in llm_judge.py requires a value in [0.74, 0.77]; passing without a correct ordinal-logit analysis is not structurally feasible.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
